name: Flávio Dias
title: Mechanical / Industrial Process Engineer
summary: Mechanical Engineer with experience in industrial processes, aluminum die casting (HPDC/LPDC), tooling development and manufacturing engineering. Led large-scale industrial initiatives including R$8M/year tooling renewal programs and MES implementation in die casting lines. Strong background in Lean Six Sigma, troubleshooting, layout optimization and data-driven decision making using Excel, Power BI and Python.

experience.title: Industrial Engineering Specialist
tasks: Led annual R$8M HPDC/LPDC mold renewal program, defining engineering criteria, investment priorities and supplier coordination.
Implemented MES monitoring in die casting machines, enabling real-time process visibility and improved defect tracking.
Developed semi-automated tooling master plan in Excel, improving planning accuracy and technical traceability.
Presented MES integration case internationally in Taiwan representing Yamaha.

experience.title: Engineering Analyst – Tooling & Stamping
tasks: Developed and improved stamping tools reducing material waste and production cost.
Created standardized operational procedures increasing process repeatability.
Implemented chemical consumption control systems using Excel.

experience.title: Industrial Analyst – Welding & Stamping
tasks: Managed welding and stamping operations for motorcycle swingarm brackets.
Led PDCA improvement initiatives eliminating chronic process defects.
Improved technical documentation and standardized work procedures supporting ISO 9001 compliance.

experience.title: Maintenance Engineering Intern
tasks: Developed maintenance manuals and inspection checklists for rotating equipment.
Built Power BI dashboard integrating OEE, MTBF and MTTR maintenance indicators.
Automated workforce planning tools using Excel.

comments: Research in dynamic modeling and gear vibration published in Springer (Qualis A2 journal).

certifications: Lean Six Sigma Green Belt — Escola DNC

publications.title: Vibration attenuation on spur gears through optimal profile modification
publications.journal: Journal of the Brazilian Society of Mechanical Sciences and Engineering (Springer)
